fix: IAM propagation delay on CodeBuild project creation

When SDK deletes and recreates the CodeBuild execution role between
failed runs, CreateProject immediately fails because IAM takes 10-30s
to propagate trust policies.

Fixes:
- patch_agentcore_yaml(): verify each stored ARN actually exists in AWS
  via 'aws iam get-role' / 'aws ecr describe-repositories' before trusting
  it. Stale ARNs (role was deleted) are set to null so SDK auto-creates
  fresh resources instead of using a ghost reference.
- launch_agentcore(): retry loop (3 attempts, 30s wait between) that
  detects IAM trust policy errors in the output and retries. Also
  regenerates the YAML before each retry (SDK deletes it on failure).
- State file: clear stale CodeBuild/ECR ARNs from previous failed run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: self-healing YAML regeneration + restore deleted config

The SDK deletes .bedrock_agentcore.yaml on failed runs, breaking
subsequent deploys with FileNotFoundError.

Fix: rewrite patch_agentcore_yaml() to always regenerate the YAML
from scratch using Python yaml.dump, pulling values from:
  1. State file (scripts/.deploy-state.json) — populated by deploy
  2. Existing YAML (if present) — preserves SDK-populated ARNs
  3. Script variables (AGENTCORE_REGION, ACCOUNT_ID, AGENT_NAME)

This means the YAML is always correct at deploy time regardless of
whether the SDK deleted or corrupted it in a previous run.

Also:
- Store SDK-populated values in state file (execution_role_arn,
  ecr_repository, codebuild_project_name, etc.) so regeneration
  works even on a fresh checkout
- Restore .bedrock_agentcore.yaml with all correct resource ARNs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: disable X-Ray observability on memory creation

enable_observability=False — X-Ray requires CloudWatch Logs as the
trace segment destination (UpdateTraceSegmentDestination) before it
can be enabled on a memory resource. We already have a full audit
pipeline (EventBridge → DynamoDB/CloudTrail/Firehose), so this SDK
observability flag is not needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: memory creation - temp file for ID, spinner, safe YAML write

Root cause: Rich SDK console writes progress to stdout; $() capture
grabbed all of it; _patch_yaml_memory_id then wrote garbage into YAML.

Fixes:
- create_agentcore_memory(): write Python script to temp file (avoids
  heredoc stdin conflicts in background subshell); run in background
  with all output redirected to log file; write ONLY memory ID to a
  separate temp file; read temp file after bg process completes
- Rich Console forced to stderr=True so stdout is always clean
- Added spinner (⠋⠙⠹...) with elapsed time counter so user knows
  the script is alive during the 1-3 min provisioning wait
- _patch_yaml_memory_id(): use yaml.safe_load/yaml.dump instead of
  regex string manipulation — safe for any value content
- _patch_yaml_memory_mode(): new helper using yaml library
- State file and YAML restored with correct memory_id aria_bank_mem-sRaj7gH5rN
  (on next run the 'reuse existing' path will be taken instantly)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use underscores in agent name — agentRuntimeName rejects hyphens

agentRuntimeName constraint: [a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,47} — no hyphens.
'aria-banking-agent' fails; 'aria_banking_agent' passes.

Changes:
- .bedrock_agentcore.yaml: rename YAML key + default_agent + name field
  from aria-banking-agent → aria_banking_agent
  (existing ECR repo, CodeBuild project, execution role ARNs preserved)
- scripts/deploy.sh: change default AGENT_NAME to aria_banking_agent
  and sanitize any user input (s/hyphen/underscore) after prompting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pre-create AgentCore memory with valid name before agentcore launch

The SDK derives memory name as '{agent_name}_mem' = 'aria-banking-agent_mem'
which fails regex [a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,47} due to hyphens. The memory_name
field in the YAML config is not used during creation.

Fix: add create_agentcore_memory() step before launch_agentcore() which:
- Creates memory with valid name 'aria_bank_mem' (no hyphens) using SDK
- Checks for existing memory before creating (idempotent)
- Patches memory_id into YAML so SDK skips creation on subsequent runs
- Falls back gracefully to STM_ONLY if creation fails

Also: add memory_id: null placeholder to YAML for regex patching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- aws.account: null — SDK autofill only triggers when the key exists

- memory_name: aria_banking_agent — memory name regex only allows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: agentcore launch - use dict format in YAML and pass --agent flag

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: redirect display functions to stderr; remove reserved AWS_REGION Lambda env var

Two bugs fixed:

1. header/step/ok/warn wrote to stdout, so any function whose return value
   was captured with $() (e.g. deploy_lambda, get_or_create_deploy_id) would
   mix progress messages into the captured value. The CLOUDTRAIL_LAMBDA_ARN
   and DYNAMODB_LAMBDA_ARN variables were getting the full 3-line progress
   output prepended to the ARN, making the EventBridge --targets JSON invalid.
   Fix: all display functions now write to stderr (>&2). Only data intended as
   a return value is written to stdout.

2. AWS_REGION is a Lambda-reserved environment variable; it is injected
   automatically by the Lambda runtime and cannot be overridden in function
   configuration. Removed it from both Lambda env var strings. The Python
   code already reads os.environ.get('AWS_REGION', 'eu-west-2') which will
   pick up the runtime-provided value correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: CloudTrail Lake - add CLI timeouts, drop process substitution, non-fatal errors

Three root-cause fixes:

1. Add --cli-connect-timeout 10 --cli-read-timeout 20 to all cloudtrail calls
   so they never hang indefinitely (previous hang was an unbounded API wait)

2. Replace 'read -r a b < <(...)' process substitution with plain variable
   capture + bash ${var%%|*} / ${var##*|} splitting — process substitution
   can hang on macOS bash 3.2 and is unnecessary here

3. Use '|' as ARN/name separator instead of space (spaces are unsafe as ARN
   fields use colons but better to be explicit)

4. Try-create-then-lookup pattern: if create fails with AlreadyExistsException
   (list call returned empty due to timeout), look up the ARN by name directly

5. All CloudTrail Lake errors are non-fatal: warn and continue — Lambda/DynamoDB
   /S3-WORM audit paths function independently

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: simplify CloudTrail Lake logic - reuse any ENABLED store, non-fatal channel

Per user feedback: if the event data store exists and is ENABLED, just use it
and move on. No more category checking, no complex RECLAIM branching.

Logic:
1. One API call captures all store data
2. Python finds any ENABLED store matching primary or fallback name -> reuse it
3. If nothing ENABLED found: check if primary name is blocked -> use fallback
4. Create with ActivityAuditLog selectors only when necessary
5. Channel creation is now non-fatal (warn + continue) so a wrong-type store
   does not block the rest of the deployment (Lambda/DynamoDB/S3 audit paths
   still function without CloudTrail Lake channel)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Branch _compile_energy_fn on functional_form (mm3 vs harmonic)
- Add functional_form field to JaxHandle dataclass

- 23 new tests: unit kernels, engine integration, OpenMM parity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(diagnostics): add reference data analysis tools for Hessians (#122)

Add q2mm/diagnostics/reference_analysis.py with:
- analyze_eigenvalues(): eigenvalue spectrum, condition number, negative count
- check_symmetry(): verify Hessian symmetry within tolerance
- compare_frequencies(): QM vs MM RMSD, MAE, max deviation, per-mode detail
- analyze_mode_coupling(): off-diagonal eigenmatrix coupling analysis
- format_hessian_report(): human-readable multi-section text report

All functions return typed dataclasses (EigenvalueAnalysis, SymmetryCheck,
FrequencyComparison, ModeCouplingAnalysis) for programmatic use.

Includes 31 tests covering all analysis functions and report generation.

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pipe+heredoc deadlock in CloudTrail Lake name detection

Using 'cmd | python3 - args <<HEREDOC' is broken in bash: the heredoc
redirects override the pipe for stdin, so python3 reads its script from
the heredoc but the pipe buffer fills up waiting for python3 to read it
→ deadlock, script hangs indefinitely.

Fix: capture aws output into a shell variable first, then pipe the
variable through 'echo $var | python3 -c "..."'. The -c form takes
the script inline so stdin is unambiguously the piped data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: rewrite CloudTrail Lake detection to be robust against all failure modes

Previous approach used fragile awk field-splitting to parse Python output,
leaving eds_name empty when parsing failed → 'Invalid length for parameter Name'.

New approach:
1. First checks state file for a previously deployed store ARN, validates it
   is still ENABLED via get-event-data-store (fast path for re-runs)
2. If no healthy stored ARN, runs a single Python heredoc against
   list-event-data-stores output to decide which name to use:
   - primary name free   → use aria-banking-audit
   - primary ENABLED     → RECLAIM path: check category, reuse or delete+fallback
   - primary blocked     → use aria-banking-audit-{DEPLOY_ID}
3. eds_name is always set before create-event-data-store is called

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle PENDING_DELETION CloudTrail Lake stores + remove account ID from names

Two changes:

1. CloudTrail Lake name conflict:
   - list-event-data-stores now scans ALL statuses (including PENDING_DELETION)
   - If store is PENDING_DELETION: use fallback name aria-banking-audit-{DEPLOY_ID}
   - If store is ENABLED with wrong category: delete it, use fallback name
   - If store is ENABLED with correct ActivityAuditLog: reuse it
   - Fallback name stored in state so teardown cleans the right resource

2. Remove account ID from resource names:
   - DEPLOY_ID (random 6-char hex) generated once per deployment, stored in state
   - S3 bucket defaults now use DEPLOY_ID suffix instead of account ID
   - Account ID is still fetched automatically for ARN construction (IAM policies etc)
     but is no longer exposed in any resource name or interactive prompt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect and replace wrong-type CloudTrail Lake event data store

update-event-data-store cannot change eventCategory — it is immutable after
creation. The only fix is to delete the wrong-type store and create a new one
with ActivityAuditLog advanced event selectors.

Changes:
- list-event-data-stores now filters Status==ENABLED (skips PENDING_DELETION)
- get-event-data-store inspects actual eventCategory of existing store
- If category != ActivityAuditLog: delete old store, set eds_arn=empty, flag
  stale_channel=true so the dependent channel is also deleted and recreated
- create-event-data-store always includes ActivityAuditLog selector
- Stale channel (pointing to old ARN) is deleted before channel recreation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use ActivityAuditLog selector for CloudTrail Lake event data store

CreateChannel requires the destination event data store to have eventCategory
= ActivityAuditLog in its advanced event selectors. The default create-event-data-store
(no selectors) creates a Management Events store which is rejected.

Changes:
- Add ActivityAuditLog advanced-event-selectors to create-event-data-store
- In the already-exists path, update-event-data-store with correct selectors
  (idempotent re-run fix for stores created without the right category)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add contributor leaderboard and contributed-by pages

New pages:
- /leaderboard — Server-rendered contributor rankings by release count.
  Queries Contributors joined to Releases. Excludes site owner by
  default with query-string toggle (?showAll=true). Fully static SSR,
  no SignalR circuit. Medal emojis for top 3, branded table.
- /contributedBy/{username} — Shows all releases contributed by a user
  in the same media-item-card grid as Movies/Series pages. Links to
  media item detail pages.

Infrastructure:
- Sitemap entries for /leaderboard and all /contributedBy/{name} URLs
- SEO meta tags, Open Graph, canonical URLs on both pages
- Leaderboard banner on /contribute page with New badge
- Code conventions documented in AGENTS.md (code-behind, no inline CSS,
  squash merge)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wait for DynamoDB ACTIVE state before enabling TTL

Table creation is async — calling update-time-to-live immediately after
create-table fails with ResourceNotFoundException while the table is still
in CREATING state. Added 'aws dynamodb wait table-exists' between the two
calls. Also applies TTL idempotently on the already-exists path so a
re-run after a partial failure completes cleanly.

commit.message:
fix(voice): stop farewell repeat + clean Ctrl+C shutdown

Goodbye repeated (core fix):
Nova Sonic generates a second farewell response because the audio input
stream remains open after completionEnd.  ARIA's farewell plays through
the speaker; even a tiny echo window (one 64 ms mic chunk before the
silence gate activates) is enough for Nova Sonic to start a new turn.
Fix: close the audio input immediately on farewell completionEnd instead
of waiting for end_session() in the finally block.
- Add _audio_input_closed flag (prevents double contentEnd)
- Add _close_audio_input() method — sends contentEnd for the mic content
  block; idempotent, swallows errors if stream is already gone
- Call _close_audio_input() in completionEnd handler when _farewell_detected
- end_session() delegates audio contentEnd to _close_audio_input()
  (only sends promptEnd + sessionEnd + stream.close now)

Ctrl+C traceback:
asyncio.run() shuts down the thread pool executor after the main task
finishes. PyAudio's stream.read() blocks in C code and cannot be quickly
joined, so a second Ctrl+C interrupts threading._shutdown → traceback.
Fix: wrap asyncio.run(run_voice_session(...)) in try/except KeyboardInterrupt
in main.py and call os._exit(0) to bypass the executor join entirely.
The '[Voice session ended]' message is printed before exiting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: use UUID5 from player name instead of sanitized name for fallback player_id

Replaces the sendspin-{sanitized_name} fallback (which needed truncation
for long names) with uuid5(NAMESPACE_DNS, name.lower()) — always 36 chars,
deterministic, no mDNS length issues. Same approach as the MAC-based path.

commit.message:
feat(voice): explicit empathy and vulnerability handling in voice preamble

Voice is the most personal channel — warmth and care must be audible.
The main system prompt has general tone and vulnerability-flag guidance,
but Nova Sonic needs concrete, voice-specific instructions to act on them
in the moment.  Add an _EMPATHY_BLOCK preamble section (applied to both
authenticated and unauthenticated sessions) covering:

EMPATHY TRIGGERS — situation-specific warm acknowledgement BEFORE action:
  - Lost/stolen card: 'I'm really sorry to hear that — let me get that
    sorted right away' then proceed immediately (no cold 'I'll need to...')
  - Suspected fraud: acknowledge how worrying it is before verifying
  - High balance / missed payment: calm, non-judgemental acknowledgement
  - Bereavement: gentle pause, offer specialist team first
  - Financial hardship: 'I hear you — let me see what options are available'
  - Audible distress: slow pace, short sentences, reassure before task

VULNERABILITY DETECTION from spoken cues (not only profile flag):
  - Distress/panic (crying, fast breathing): slow pace, one step at a time
  - Confusion/repetition: simplest language, confirm understanding each step
  - Third-party pressure (coached caller): halt irreversible actions,
    escalate — 'I want to make sure everything is done safely for you'
  - Mid-call disclosure (illness, mental health, bereavement, debt):
    treat as refer_to_specialist immediately, offer support team

WARM ACKNOWLEDGMENT RULE:
  One warm human sentence FIRST on any distressing call, before any task,
  security check, or tool call. Never jump to 'I'll need to verify...'
  when the customer has just shared something upsetting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(voice): card-digits prompt + goodbye session-end reliability

Issue 1 — ARIA asking customer for card last-4 digits it already knows
- Tool docstrings say 'card_last_four must be retrieved from the PII vault'
  which Nova Sonic follows literally, causing it to ask the customer
- Add CARD QUERIES — VOICE OVERRIDE block to authenticated voice preamble:
  * After get_customer_details, all card_last_four values are already known
  * NEVER call pii_vault_retrieve for card_last_four; use profile directly
  * ONE card of the requested type → use its last_four directly, tell
    customer which card is being accessed (e.g. 'your Visa Debit ending 4821')
  * MULTIPLE cards of same type → list each by scheme + last_four and ask
    which one the customer means
  * 'Confirm the card' = TELL customer which card, NOT ask for digits

Issue 2 — goodbye did not close the session; 'started a new one'
Root cause: after _farewell_detected=True, capture_audio() kept sending
real mic audio. Nova Sonic picked up ARIA's farewell echo / ambient noise
as a new user turn and started generating a fresh response.
Fix: in capture_audio(), when _farewell_detected is True, always replace
mic chunks with silence before sending. This prevents any new Nova Sonic
turn from being triggered after the customer says goodbye.

Ctrl+C traceback:
- Wrap gather/end_session in individual try-except blocks in the finally
  clause so asyncio.CancelledError during teardown does not surface as
  an unhandled exception in the terminal

commit.message:
feat(voice): smart echo gate + proper barge-in interrupt handling

- Add _compute_rms() helper (struct/math) for per-chunk energy measurement
- Add _BARGE_IN_THRESHOLD constant (NOVA_BARGE_IN_THRESHOLD env var, default 800)
  - Value 0 = always send real mic audio (headphone users, no muting)
  - Value >0 = energy-based gate: pass through if rms >= threshold (barge-in),
    suppress low-energy audio (echo) otherwise
- Replace hard mute in capture_audio() with smart energy-based gate:
  during ARIA playback, chunks with rms >= threshold pass through so Nova
  Sonic can detect barge-in; low-energy echo chunks are suppressed
- Fix interrupt detection in _handle_event():
  - Was: naive string search ('interrupted' in text.lower())
  - Now: mirrors React client — textOutput role=ASSISTANT, content starts
    with '{', json.loads, check evt['interrupted'] is True
- Add _handle_interrupt() method:
  - Drains _audio_output_queue immediately (mirrors bargeIn()/clearBuffer())
  - Clears _aria_buf (discard in-progress ARIA text for interrupted turn)
  - Resets _silence_until = 0.0 so echo gate reopens immediately
  - Prints '[Customer interrupted — listening...]'
- Update startup banner to display current barge-in threshold and hint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: align CHANGELOG with actual export posture

- modeResolver: correctly documented as exported via core/prototyping barrel
- evidence/providers/browserQa: documented as internal, not yet exported
- Removed misleading Changed section (ProviderRegistry is new in v1.7.5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(voice): detect farewell and end session cleanly; drain farewell audio

Previously when the customer said 'goodbye', ARIA responded correctly
but the session kept looping because:
  1. No code ever ended the session on farewell
  2. The echo gate couldn't fully prevent ARIA's goodbye audio from
     being re-transcribed as another customer utterance
  3. end_session() was guarded by 'if not is_active: return' so it
     never actually sent promptEnd/sessionEnd events

Changes:
- _FAREWELL_PHRASES: frozenset of goodbye/farewell phrases
- _farewell_detected / _session_ended instance flags on the session
- _handle_event USER branch: detect farewell phrase → set flag
- completionEnd: if farewell detected → set is_active=False (stops
  _process_responses loop and hence response_task)
- end_session(): guard changed from is_active to _session_ended flag
  so it correctly sends promptEnd/sessionEnd even after is_active=False
- play_audio(): loop condition changed to drain remaining queue after
  is_active=False so farewell audio plays completely before exit
- run_voice_session() finally block: cancel capture immediately,
  wait up to 8s for playback to drain before tearing down the stream

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prototype-safe Maps, atomic replace(), CHANGELOG internal-only wording

- registry: switch CAPABILITY_METHOD_MAP from Record to Map (prototype-safe)
- registry: extract validateProvider(), make replace() atomic (validate before mutate)
- runner: use Object.hasOwn() instead of 'in' for prototype-safe tier check
- CHANGELOG: clarify all v1.7.5 modules are internal/foundation-only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: duplicate provider guard, tier runtime validation, async error propagation

- ProviderRegistry: reject duplicate name on register(), add replace() API
- runner: validate config.tier at runtime before indexing TIER_PHASES
- runner: wrap sync throw in Promise.reject for proper async error propagation
- Tests: add tier validation, duplicate registration, and replace() tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(voice): inject SESSION_START kickoff after tasks start to trigger ARIA banking persona

The voice agent was behaving as a generic assistant because it had no
trigger to call get_customer_details and greet the customer by name.
The chat agent achieves this via a SESSION_START message sent as the
first user message; the voice agent now mirrors that with an equivalent
kickoff TEXT block.

Key changes:
- ARIANovaSonicSession now stores authenticated/customer_id
- New send_kickoff() method sends the SESSION_START trigger as a USER
  TEXT content block after all tasks (esp. _process_responses) are
  already running — ensuring toolUse responses are handled correctly
- run_voice_session() calls send_kickoff() immediately after launching
  response/playback/capture tasks
- Kickoff text matches _build_session_start() from main.py for parity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use custom StaticResolver + SDK default auth_schemes for Nova Sonic

Two credential config bugs fixed:

1. auth_schemes dict key mismatch
   The SDK auth lookup uses ShapeID("aws.auth#sigv4") as the key in its
   internal registry. Passing a plain string key "aws.auth#sigv4" never
   matches, so no auth scheme is found and the connection hangs
   indefinitely (silent auth failure). Fix: don't override auth_schemes /
   auth_scheme_resolver — let the SDK defaults take effect.

2. Direct credential fields not wired to resolver
   Passing aws_access_key_id/secret/token directly to Config doesn't
   automatically create a StaticCredentialsResolver in this SDK version.
   Fix: create an explicit _StaticResolver wrapping boto3 frozen creds
   and pass it as aws_credentials_identity_resolver.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use get_frozen_credentials() not .resolve() for boto3 creds

boto3 Credentials objects expose get_frozen_credentials() to obtain
the resolved access_key/secret_key/token — not .resolve().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: replace strands bidi SDK with direct Nova Sonic 2 API

Complete rewrite of voice_agent.py using aws_sdk_bedrock_runtime directly,
following the official AWS Nova 2 Sonic example:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/nova/latest/nova2-userguide/sonic-getting-started.html

Root cause of previous echo issue: the strands bidi SDK abstracted away the
SPECULATIVE/FINAL stage distinction and the echo gate did not fire in time
because the SDK's audio pipeline ran independently of our gate flag.

Key changes:
- Direct BedrockRuntimeClient + InvokeModelWithBidirectionalStream
- Credentials: boto3 session credentials injected via Config params
  (supports profiles, role assumption, env vars — full boto3 chain)
- Display logic: display_assistant_text=True only on SPECULATIVE contentStart;
  USER textOutput always shown (no is_final ambiguity)
- Echo gate: play_audio() updates _silence_until = now + chunk_duration +
  ECHO_TAIL_SECS before each write; capture_audio() sends silence when
  time.monotonic() < _silence_until (default tail = 0.8s, ECHO_GATE_TAIL_SECS)
- Audio: input 16kHz / output 24kHz (correct Nova 2 Sonic rates)
- Tools: all 20 ARIA Strands tools wired as Nova Sonic toolSpec entries;
  session_id stripped from schemas and injected automatically at execution
- Tool execution: asyncio.to_thread() so sync tool functions don't block loop
- Session lifecycle: start_session → response+playback+capture tasks →
  Ctrl-C/goodbye → end_session (promptEnd + sessionEnd + stream close)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: version bump to 1.7.5, use vi.doMock for guaranteed mock ordering

- Bump version: package.json, CHANGELOG.md, docs/examples toolVersion
- CHANGELOG: add v1.7.5 entries for all spec-0028 features
- sliceRevertIndependence: replace vi.mock with vi.resetModules + vi.doMock
  + dynamic import to guarantee mock applies before module evaluation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct user transcript rule + add half-duplex echo gate

Two root causes fixed:

1. User speech transcript not displayed
   Nova Sonic sends USER speech with generationStage=FINAL, which maps
   to is_final=True in the strands SDK. Our previous rule showed only
   is_final=False for user speech, so all user transcripts were skipped.
   Fix: role=user + is_final=True → print Customer line (confirmed final
   transcript from Nova Sonic). role=user + is_final=False → skip (partial).

2. ARIA 'hello, hello' loop (acoustic echo)
   Microphone captures ARIA's speaker output → Nova Sonic transcribes it
   as user speech → agent generates another greeting → repeats forever.
   Fix: _EchoGate + _GatedAudioInput/_GatedAudioOutput implement half-duplex
   gating. The output marks the gate on every audio chunk; the input sends
   silence (zero bytes) for TAIL_SECS (default 0.8s) after the last chunk,
   letting the echo decay before the mic is live again.
   ECHO_GATE_TAIL_SECS env var allows tuning (default 0.8s).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

style: format registry.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard unknown capability in ProviderRegistry.register

Add runtime check for unrecognized capability keys before iterating
CAPABILITY_METHOD_MAP, preventing TypeError on JS callers with
unvalidated input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: display SPECULATIVE stage for ARIA, skip FINAL echo

Root cause confirmed from AWS sample (genai-quickstart-pocs hotel POC):

Nova Sonic sends text output in two generation stages:

  SPECULATIVE (is_final=False in strands):
    The actual AI response text. This is what ARIA is saying.
    → SHOW for role=assistant (accumulate, flush on BidiResponseCompleteEvent)

  FINAL (is_final=True in strands):
    A post-hoc transcription of the audio that was played back.
    This is an audio echo of the TTS output — NOT the user speaking.
    → SKIP entirely for both roles

For USER speech: contentStart has no additionalModelFields so
_generation_stage stays None → is_final=False always for real user
speech → display these as Customer turns.

Previous code had the logic inverted: it was showing is_final=True
(FINAL echo) and skipping is_final=False (real SPECULATIVE AI text),
causing the alternating ARIA/Customer echo pattern.

Reference: nova_sonic_reservation_agent_demo.py lines 710-730:
  display_assistant_text = True  when generationStage == SPECULATIVE
  display_assistant_text = False when generationStage != SPECULATIVE
  USER text always displayed (is always SPECULATIVE/None stage)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: defensive coding — exhaustiveness guard, capability-method validation, runner comment

- modeResolver: add default case with never exhaustiveness check
- runner: clarify foundation-only comment for phase execution
- ProviderRegistry: validate that declared capabilities have matching methods
- BrowserProvider: add optional stubs for interaction/visual/accessibility
- Tests: provide matching method stubs for full-capability providers
- Tests: add capability-method validation test cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: accumulate ARIA transcript, flush on response complete

Nova Sonic emits one BidiTranscriptStreamEvent per sentence — each
textOutput chunk arrives as a separate event with its own role.
Printing each event individually broke ARIA's response across multiple
alternating ARIA:/Customer: lines.

New approach:
- ASSISTANT chunks (is_final=True) are appended to _aria_buf
- Buffer is flushed as one complete block on BidiResponseCompleteEvent
- USER utterances (is_final=True) are printed immediately, with any
  pending ARIA text flushed first to preserve correct ARIA→Customer order
- Speculative-stage events (is_final=False) are ignored
- BidiInterruptionEvent clears the partial buffer (user barged in)
- _flush_aria() also called in stop() to catch any trailing text

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
